content: I'm training for the Chicago Marathon this October and I'm struggling with my long run nutrition strategy. Any advice for someone who's never run more than a half marathon before?
That's a big leap in distance! For marathon training, you'll want to practice your fueling strategy during your long runs. What have you been using so far?
Just water and sometimes a banana. I usually DJ at clubs on weekends until 2am, so my recovery nutrition has been pretty inconsistent. I'm trying to get better about it since I turned 57 last month.
Age is just a number! For marathon training, you'll want to start fueling with carbohydrates about 30-45 minutes into your long runs. Have you tried energy gels or chews?

I haven't, but I'm open to trying. I volunteer at the animal shelter on Saturday mornings, so I usually do my long runs Sunday afternoons. It's hard to fit everything in with my music production schedule.

Sunday afternoon runs could work well for practicing your race-day nutrition timing. Start with 30-60 grams of carbs per hour and see how your stomach tolerates it. What's your typical Sunday run distance right now?
About 10-12 miles. I've been playing piano since I was 5, so I'm used to disciplined practice, but marathon training is a whole different beast. My knees have been bothering me a bit lately.
Piano practice definitely builds discipline! For your knees, make sure you're strengthening your hips and glutes too - they play a big role in knee stability. Have you tried any cross-training?
I've been doing some swimming, actually. I started home brewing beer last year and my brewing partner Carlos is a physical therapist. He's been giving me exercises for my IT band.
That's perfect - having a physical therapist as a brewing partner! For your long runs, also practice your hydration strategy. How much are you drinking during your 10-12 mile runs?
Usually just a handheld water bottle with about 16oz. I'm trying to figure out if I should carry more for the marathon or rely on the aid stations. The Chicago Marathon has them every 2 miles.
